name: fastmcp
description: Build MCP servers in Python. Originally by jlowin, now maintained under PrefectHQ/fastmcp. The fastmcp framework turns a Python class into an MCP-compatible stdio server with @mcp.tool decorators. We test the install on Claude Desktop, Cursor, Cline, and Windsurf.

claude-desktop.notes: Replace <server.py> with the path to your fastmcp server module. Claude Desktop picks the path up from the mcpServers.<name>.args list verbatim.

gotchas: uvx is the canonical runner for fastmcp in 2026 (it replaced pipx + virtualenv for one-off CLIs). Install uv first: https://docs.astral.sh/uv/

fastmcp.run() expects a module path, not a script path. The install command is therefore uvx fastmcp run path/to/your/server.py — there's no separate 'install the server' step because fastmcp reads the file every boot.
If your server uses third-party Python deps, put them in a pyproject.toml next to your server.py and add --with to the args: uvx --with pandas fastmcp run ./server.py.
On macOS, uvx is at /Users/$USER/.local/bin/uvx after install. If a client can't find it, set the command to the full path or add ~/.local/bin to the client's PATH.
notes: Universal install via uvx means there is no per-platform divergence — the mcpServers config is identical across Claude Desktop, Cursor, Cline, and Windsurf. Tested on each with the bundled examples/server.py from the upstream repo.
